name: Hispanic Heritage Month
description: From the Cry of Dolores to Hamilton — history, holidays, and trailblazers behind National Hispanic Heritage Month.

questions.q: When is National Hispanic Heritage Month celebrated each year in the United States?
Which U.S. president signed the original 1968 legislation establishing Hispanic Heritage Week?
Hispanic Heritage Week was expanded to a full month in 1988 under which president?
September 15 is significant because it's the anniversary of independence for which set of countries?

source.original_a: Costa Rica, El Salvador, Guatemala, Honduras, and Nicaragua
source.notes: Costa Rica, El Salvador, Guatemala, Honduras, and Nicaragua all gained independence from Spain on September 15, 1821.

questions.q: Which event in 1810 — commemorated each September 16 — sparked the Mexican War of Independence?

source.notes: Father Miguel Hidalgo's 'Grito de Dolores' on September 16, 1810.

questions.q: On what date does Mexico celebrate its Independence Day?

source.notes: Often confused with Cinco de Mayo (May 5), which marks the Battle of Puebla, not independence.

questions.q: Chile celebrates its independence on which date during Hispanic Heritage Month?
Which holiday during Hispanic Heritage Month is also known as 'Día de la Raza' in Latin America?

source.notes: Observed October 12; many countries now also call it Día de la Hispanidad or Día de los Pueblos Originarios.

questions.q: Who was the first Latina astronaut to travel to space?

source.notes: Flew aboard Discovery in 1993; later became director of NASA's Johnson Space Center.

questions.q: Who co-founded the National Farm Workers Association (later the United Farm Workers) in 1962?

source.notes: Co-founded with Dolores Huerta. His birthday (March 31) is a US federal commemorative day.

questions.q: Who is the first Latina Justice on the U.S. Supreme Court?

source.notes: Confirmed in 2009. She is of Puerto Rican heritage.

questions.q: Who created the Broadway musicals 'Hamilton' and 'In the Heights'?
Which Venezuelan-American fashion designer launched her namesake brand in New York in 1981?
Which Dominican-born designer dressed first ladies and movie stars from the 1960s through the 2010s?

source.notes: Born in Santo Domingo, Dominican Republic; died 2014.

questions.q: Which singer is known as the 'Queen of Tejano' music?

source.notes: Selena Quintanilla-Pérez. Murdered in 1995 at age 23.

questions.q: Linda Alvarado broke barriers as the first Hispanic owner in which major sport?

source.notes: Co-owner of the Colorado Rockies since 1991.

questions.q: Who was the first U.S. Air Force Latina military pilot?

source.notes: Puerto Rican-born; broke the barrier in 1981.

questions.q: Author Sandra Cisneros is best known for which 1984 coming-of-age novel?
choices: In the Time of the Butterflies

source.notes: Cisneros became the first Latina MacArthur Fellow in 1995.

questions.q: Approximately what share of MLB players were of Hispanic/Latino heritage on Opening Day 2023?
How many Latino service members have been awarded the U.S. Medal of Honor?

source.notes: 61 as of recent counts; majority from Army (46), then Marines (13).
